seoTitle: How to Uninstall Microsoft Excel on Windows 11
metaDescription: Remove or modify Microsoft Excel on Windows 11 using the built-in Apps settings, and learn why bundled Office suites can't be partially uninstalled.
intro: Whether you're freeing up disk space, clearing out a corrupted install before reinstalling, or moving fully to cloud-based spreadsheets, removing Excel from Windows 11 is straightforward through the built-in Settings app. There's one important catch worth knowing beforehand if Excel came bundled with the rest of Microsoft 365.

steps.heading: Open Windows Settings
steps.body: Go to the modern Windows 11 Settings app and navigate to the Apps section, which centralizes management for all installed software.

steps.heading: Find your installed Office apps
steps.body: Locate Microsoft Excel or your broader Microsoft 365 suite in the list of installed applications.

steps.heading: Choose uninstall or modify
steps.body: Select the app and choose to uninstall, or choose modify if you want to adjust which specific Office components are installed rather than removing everything.

steps.heading: Understand suite bundling limits
steps.body: If Excel was installed as part of a bundle like Microsoft 365 or Office Home & Student, Windows 11 treats the whole bundle as one entity, so you can't strip out just Excel while keeping Word or PowerPoint.

steps.heading: Restart if prompted
steps.body: Complete any system restart Windows requests after uninstalling to ensure leftover files and registry entries are cleared properly.

faq.question: Can I uninstall just Excel and keep Word and PowerPoint?
faq.answer: Not if they were installed together as part of a Microsoft 365 or Office bundle, since Windows 11 manages the suite as a single entity.

faq.question: Will uninstalling Excel delete my saved spreadsheet files?
faq.answer: No, your personal documents are not deleted when you uninstall the application itself, only the software.

faq.question: What if uninstalling leaves errors or leftover files?
faq.answer: Microsoft provides official cleanup support tools designed to remove residual files after a standard uninstall doesn't fully clear the system.

faq.question: Do I need administrator access to uninstall Excel?
faq.answer: Yes, removing installed software on Windows 11 typically requires administrator permissions on the device.
